title: "Replication value of 0" error inside unreachable code
body:
Essentially the same issue as #2866 #2625 and potentially more, Verilator complains about replication value of 0 inside unreachable code (example from [here](
https://github.com/alexforencich/verilog-axi/blob/38915fb5330cb8270b454afc0140a94489dc56db/rtl/axi_adapter_wr.v#L356-L358)):

```verilog
parameter EXPAND = M_STRB_WIDTH > S_STRB_WIDTH;
...
    end else if (EXPAND) begin
        // master output is wider; merge writes
        m_axi_wdata_int = {(M_WORD_WIDTH/S_WORD_WIDTH){s_axi_wdata}};
```

```
%Error: /local/home/pengxu/work-local/enzian-pio-nic/hw/deps/verilog-axi/rtl/axi_adapter_wr.v:358:55: Replication value of 0 is only legal under a concatenation (IEEE 1800-2017 11.4.12.1)  
                                                                                                    : ... note: In instance 'PioNicEngine.axiAdapter.axi_adapter_wr_inst'
  358 |         m_axi_wdata_int = {(M_WORD_WIDTH/S_WORD_WIDTH){s_axi_wdata}};                 
      |                                                       ^
```

I would propose we take the same route as in #2754 and downgrade this error into a warning, maybe something called `ZEROREPL`; I can work on a PR if that's desirable.
